title: Vitakka and the "application" (abhiniropana) gloss: does the reading harden by layer?
verdict: NULL on the quantitative primary metric. The within-vitakka application-over-discursive ratio does NOT rise monotonically across mula, attha, tika (it is 0.213, 0.510, 0.441: a rise then a fall), and the piti negative control fails twice (it carries the same low-mula, peak-attha, fall-tika shape with a larger mula-to-attha jump, and the piti defining-frame count is non-empty at mula density). The predicted layer-wise hardening is therefore not measurable as a vitakka-specific effect; the ratio movement is consistent with a register and length shift over the target subset. The qualitative first-appearance axis (whether the definitional-head form appears first in commentary) is left UNCODED and is reported as not-yet-established, not as part of the null.

analyst.control_check: The control did NOT stay flat and it moved with the signal, so the effect is an artifact per the pre-committed falsifier (if piti moves with vitakka the effect is artifact regardless of vitakka numbers). Q4 piti app over disc ratio: mula 0.304, attha 0.813, tika 0.520, the same low-mula peak-attha fall-tika shape as vitakka, and the piti mula-to-attha jump of about 0.51 exceeds vitakka about 0.30. Secondary control also fails: Q8 piti defining-frame rows are mula 11, attha 12, tika 6, other 17, not empty and not vitakka-specific, so the defining-frame skeleton is commentary boilerplate that also attaches to piti at mula density.

key_numbers.name: Q4 mula piti negctrl app/disc ratio
Q4 attha piti negctrl app/disc ratio
Q4 tika piti negctrl app/disc ratio
Q2 app per mchar within target
Q2 disc per mchar within target
Q8 piti defining-frame rows
key_numbers.value: mula 11, attha 12, tika 6, other 17 not flat

key_numbers.name: Q6 vitakka app defframe mula work-hits
Q9 diacritic zero-guard
key_numbers.value: ERROR statement timeout, non-zero confirmed via Q5 rows

analyst.codebook: Unit: one passage from Q5_defining_frame_rows_to_code (73 rows), keyed on row id (e.g. cst-abh01a.att-20_p002). Coders see only original_head; work_slug, work_role, layer MASKED (layer-blind). PRIMARY definitional_head (1/0): 1 = abhiniropana/aropana (or kin abhiropana, aropana, anuropana) functions as a DEFINITIONAL head-noun glossing vitakka, predicated of vitakka as its meaning, characteristic, or essence. Surface marks: abhiniropanalakkhano vitakko; vitakko arammane cittassa abhiniropana; vitakkassa abhiniropanalakkhanam; or abhiniropana coupled with lakkhana, rasa, paccupatthana, attho, nama, ti-vuccati as the gloss OF vitakka. 0 = mere co-mention or list-item: (a) the MN 117 synonym chain takko vitakko sankappo to cetaso abhiniropana vacisankharo with no defined-as predication, (b) abhiniropana/aropana predicated of a different subject such as vadaropana mounting-a-debate or planting/raising, (c) passing narrative mention. SECONDARY application_sense (1/0) on the abhiniropana/aropana token, 50-row sense sample as a polysemy guard: 1 = mental application-to-object, lifting-the-mind-onto-the-object; 0 = unrelated aropana sense such as planting, raising, mounting a debate. Adjudication: compute Cohen kappa on definitional_head; require kappa at least 0.6 to proceed, else study invalid; only adjudicated definitional_head=1 rows feed the Q6 first-appearance claim. Coders must not infer layer from vocabulary or register.
analyst.density_finding: Q3 within-vitakka APPLICATION over DISCURSIVE passage-presence ratio is non-monotonic: mula 0.213, attha 0.510, tika 0.441. Real rise mula-to-attha of about 0.30 (the predicted largest jump), then a fall into tika (0.510 to 0.441). The monotone mula then attha then tika shape H1 requires is absent; the H1 falsifier names this outcome. Q2 per-Mchar within-target densities: application 4.729 mula, 70.473 attha, 52.624 tika; discursive 22.204 mula, 138.127 attha, 119.282 tika. Application and discursive move together (jump into commentary, dip attha-to-tika), the signature of a register or length shift over the target subset (mula 4.86 Mchar vs attha 0.71 Mchar), not a vitakka-specific hardening.

skeptics.refutation: The null survives on its primary quantitative metric (control failure + register confound both confirmed), but the verdict must be downgraded from a clean kill of the whole study to a scoped caveat: the artifact attribution is proven only for the Q3/Q4 ratio metric, while the definitional-head first-appearance axis is unrefuted-but-uncoded and was wrongly absorbed into the null. The mula rows show the H1-confirmation pattern (list-item in mula, definitional-head only in attha/tika), not the H1 falsifier, so that sub-claim cannot be reported as dead.
required_caveats: The quantitative ratio-shape result (Q3) is a confirmed measurement artifact: the piti negative control (Q4) moves with the same low-mula/peak-attha/fall-tika shape and a larger mula-to-attha jump (0.509 vs 0.297); per the pre-committed falsifier the effect is declared artifact regardless of the vitakka numbers.

The Q3 mula ratio is uninterpretable on its own: passage-presence counting gives a 224k-char Patisambhidamagga/Niddesa block the same weight as a 176-char Atthasalini gloss line (mula target passages average 8430 chars vs attha 721 chars), so the low mula ratio reflects giant-passage dilution, not application-gloss rarity.

The null is established for the QUANTITATIVE primary metric only. The qualitative defining-frame first-appearance axis (Q5/Q6) is unrefuted but uncoded: in genuine mula every abhiniropana co-occurrence is the MN117 synonym-chain list-item (codebook=0) while the definitional-head form appears only in attha/tika rows. Do not fold this axis into the null. It must be coded (Cohen kappa >= 0.6) before any first-appearance claim is made or denied.

The secondary piti defining-frame control (Q8) is flat-and-nonzero (mula 11, attha 12, tika 6), an ambiguous outcome, not a clean confirmation that the defining-frame skeleton is commentary boilerplate. The Q8 regex only requires piti + application-token + frame-token to co-occur in a passage, not abhiniropana predicated of piti; nonzero counts are expected because piti sits inside the same jhana-factor lakkhana lists.
Layer is a work_slug lookup, not independent chronology; pli-vism is bucketed as attha though work_role=mula. Report results with and without pli-vism (defframe attha holds 20 of 25 hits without it).

Q9 diacritic zero-guard returned a statement-timeout error and did not complete; diacritic integrity is only indirectly inferred from nonzero Q5 rows. The zero-guard must be re-run to clear the WHOLE-STUDY-INVALID condition before any reuse of these numbers.

State the null cleanly on Q3 grounds (within-vitakka app/disc ratio is non-monotonic mula 0.213 / attha 0.510 / tika 0.441; the predicted monotone hardening is absent). Do NOT claim the piti control 'moved with' vitakka: vitakka does not rise monotonically and piti is itself non-monotonic, so the pre-committed slope-match artifact test does not apply. The piti result shows only that the attha bump is not vitakka-specific.

Report bootstrap 95% CIs on every ratio. The attha and tika ratios rest on tiny denominators (piti attha disc=16/app=13, tika disc=25/app=13; vitakka attha app=50/disc=98). Without CIs no magnitude comparison (e.g. '0.51 exceeds 0.30') is defensible.

Mark the defining-frame / first-appearance result as NOT YET ESTABLISHED: coding is required (needs_coding=true), Cohen kappa>=0.6 has not been computed, the 50-row application-sense audit has not run, and per the protocol's own falsifier the definitional claim is invalid until kappa passes. The Q5/Q6 mula 'defframe_hits' are unadjudicated regex co-occurrences, not confirmed definitions.
Note Q9 errored (statement timeout); the diacritic zero-guard is only indirectly satisfied via non-empty Q5 rows, not by the committed guard query. State the guard as partially unverified.

Do not present this as a discovery. The substantive position (abhiniropana-as-application is the commentarial gloss first systematized in Atthasalini Dhs-a / Visuddhimagga IV; the MN117 / Dhammasangani synonym chain is a list-item, not a definition of vitakka) is the established secondary-literature reading and a live unresolved scholarly dispute. Cite it as corroboration in density terms, not as new.

Keep strictly corpus-linguistic. Claim only that the lexical-definitional surface pattern 'abhiniropanalakkhano vitakko' is absent from the canonical mula subset held in this corpus and present in attha; do NOT claim the canon did or did not treat vitakka as application (doctrinal, out of scope per scope_limits).

Report the pli-vism sensitivity explicitly: pli-vism is shelved-as-mula but counted as attha here and contributes 5 of the attha defframe hits (Q6); the attha definitional density is partly a convention artifact, not a fact about layer chronology (stratum is a work_slug lookup, T1).

page_spec.abstract: A familiar reading holds that the 'application of mind to its object' sense of vitakka, carried by the word abhiniropana and its kin, is a commentarial development rather than a canonical one. This study tests a corpus-linguistic version: within passages mentioning vitakka or vicara, does the application vocabulary harden against the discursive vocabulary across the mula, attha, and tika layers, in a way the control term piti does not share? The test fails. The within-vitakka application-over-discursive ratio is 0.213 in mula, 0.510 in attha, 0.441 in tika: a rise then a fall, not the predicted monotone climb. The piti control carries the same low-mula, peak-attha, fall-tika shape with a larger mula-to-attha jump, and a pre-committed falsifier declares the effect an artifact whenever the control moves with the signal. The verdict on the quantitative metric is null. A separate qualitative axis, whether the definitional-head form appears first in commentary, is left uncoded and is reported as open, not as part of the null.

sections.heading: The question and why it is a fair test
paragraphs: The anchor term is vitakka and vicara; all densities are computed only inside passages containing it, so the comparison is internal to the gloss subject. Two vocabularies are tracked: an APPLICATION field (abhiniropana, aropana, anumajjana, anusancarana, upanijjhana, patitthapana and kin) and a DISCURSIVE field (papanca, sankappa, takka, vitakka, cinta, cetana). The hypothesis predicts the application field rises relative to the discursive field across mula, attha, tika.

The design is built to fail three ways before it can be believed. The layer ratio must be monotone or it is falsified. The same measurement is run on piti, a co-present jhana factor with no application-gloss debate attached; if piti rises with the same shape, the vitakka rise is generic register. All counts are per-character over the target subset, so longer commentary passages cannot inflate raw counts.

Layer is a lookup on the work identifier, so a mula-versus-attha split is true by construction and carries no evidentiary weight on its own. The diacritic zero-guard that was to confirm no search term silently returned zero did not complete (statement timeout), so diacritic integrity is only indirectly verified through the returned rows and remains a partially open check.

sections.heading: What the canon shows
paragraphs: In the mula layer the application vocabulary inside vitakka passages is sparse and appears as list items, not definitions. The defining-frame harvest returns ten mula co-occurrences, every one an enumeration or synonym chain: four Dhammasangani dhamma-list rows, three Patisambhidamagga blocks, two Petakopadesa blocks, one Niddesa gloss, plus MN 117's 'takko vitakko sankappo ... cetaso abhiniropana' chain where abhiniropana is one member, not a predication of vitakka.

The within-target densities move jointly. Application per Mchar is 4.729 in mula, 70.473 in attha, 52.624 in tika; discursive per Mchar is 22.204 in mula, 138.127 in attha, 119.282 in tika. Both fields jump into commentary together and dip from attha to tika together. That joint movement is the signature of a register and length change over the subset, not of one vocabulary hardening against the other.

Passage size differs sharply by layer. The mula target subset averages roughly 8,430 characters per passage while the attha subset averages roughly 721, so passage-presence counting weights one giant mula block the same as a one-line attha gloss. The low mula ratio is partly that dilution.

sections.heading: What the commentary does, and the falsifiable core
paragraphs: In commentary the definitional-head form is present and stereotyped. The line 'Vitakkassa abhiniropanalakkhanam, vicarassa anumajjanalakkhanam, pitiya pharanalakkhanam ...' recurs verbatim across the Digha, Majjhima, Anguttara, and Khuddaka commentaries, and the fuller 'arammane cittassa abhiniropanalakkhano vitakko' recurs throughout. The surface pattern the secondary literature treats as the commentarial gloss is dense in attha and tika; this is stated only in density terms, as corroboration of a known reading.

The falsifiable core is the ratio shape plus the control. The shape is non-monotone, which the hypothesis names as a falsifier. The control then settles it: the pre-committed rule is that if the piti ratio moves with the same shape as vitakka, the effect is an artifact regardless of the vitakka numbers. Piti's ratio is 0.304, 0.813, 0.520, the same low-mula, peak-attha, fall-tika shape, with a mula-to-attha jump of about 0.51 that exceeds vitakka's of about 0.30.

The secondary piti control reinforces this. The defining-frame skeleton, expected to be empty or flat on piti, is 11 rows in mula, 12 in attha, 6 in tika. It attaches to piti at mula density because piti sits inside the same jhana-factor characteristic lists, so the frame is commentary scaffolding rather than a vitakka-specific definitional act.

table.caption: Within-anchor application-over-discursive ratio by layer, with the piti negative control (Q3 and Q4) and the secondary piti defining-frame control (Q8). Source: _raw.json.

sections.heading: The negative-control result
paragraphs: The negative control did not stay flat and it moved with the signal. By the program rule that a finding stands only if its control did not move with it, the quantitative finding does not stand.

On phrasing: vitakka does not itself rise monotonically, so the strict slope-match version of the artifact test does not apply; both series are non-monotone. What the piti result shows is that the attha bump is not vitakka-specific. The same bump, larger, appears on a term with no application-gloss debate attached. That is sufficient to deny a vitakka-specific effect, which is all the null requires.

sections.heading: What it means and what it does not mean
paragraphs: It means the within-vitakka balance of application versus discursive vocabulary does not harden monotonically by layer in this corpus, and the commentary-layer bump in application vocabulary is matched and exceeded by the same bump on piti. The measurable movement is consistent with commentary being a denser, shorter, more formulaic register over the subset.

It does not mean the application reading is canonical, nor that it is absent from the canon. The first-appearance question is a separate axis this run does not settle. In the returned rows every mula abhiniropana co-occurrence is a list item or synonym chain while the predicated definitional form clusters in attha and tika, but distinguishing a definition from a co-mention requires blind coding. Cohen kappa of at least 0.6 has not been computed, and the 50-row application-sense audit guarding the unrelated senses of aropana (planting, raising, mounting a debate) has not run. Until that coding passes, the first-appearance claim is neither confirmed nor denied and must not be folded into the null.
It settles nothing doctrinal. This is a statement about where a lexical and definitional surface pattern sits in the held text by layer, not about what the Buddha meant by vitakka.

Two limits bound the numbers. The attha and tika ratios rest on small denominators (vitakka attha 50 over 98; piti attha 13 over 16; piti tika 13 over 25), so magnitude comparisons such as '0.51 exceeds 0.30' need bootstrap confidence intervals before they carry weight. And pli-vism is shelved as mula by work role but counted as attha here by convention; it contributes 5 of the 25 attha defining-frame hits, so the attha definitional density is partly a convention choice. Without pli-vism the attha count is 20 of 25 and the direction is unchanged.

sections.heading: Auditability
paragraphs: Every count re-derives from the two committed files. _protocol.json holds the frozen lexica, the layer definitions (mula is work role mula excluding pli-vism; attha is any work slug ending in -attha plus pli-vism; tika is any work slug ending in -tika), the enumeration SQL, the controls, and the falsifiers. _raw.json holds the executed results Q0 through Q9.

The 73 Q5 rows are inspectable by passage id: cst-s0101a.att-dn1_1_p154 for the stereotyped atthakatha characteristic line, mn117 for the canonical synonym chain, cst-abh01m.mul-016 for a Dhammasangani enumeration. Numbers here are read directly from result rows; none are recomputed except per-passage average sizes, which are Q1 and Q2 character sums divided by row counts. Q9, the diacritic zero-guard, returned a statement-timeout error and did not complete; it must be re-run before these numbers are reused.

page_spec.verdict: NULL on the quantitative primary metric. The within-vitakka application-over-discursive ratio is non-monotonic (mula 0.213, attha 0.510, tika 0.441), absent the predicted monotone hardening, and the piti negative control fails twice (the same low-mula/peak-attha/fall-tika shape with a larger mula-to-attha jump, plus a non-empty mula-density defining-frame count). The qualitative first-appearance axis is uncoded and reported as not-yet-established, not as part of the null.
scope_limits: Corpus-linguistic only: dates when a lexical and definitional surface pattern appears in the held Pali by layer; cannot settle what the Buddha meant by vitakka or adjudicate the thinking-versus-application debate.
Layer is a lookup on the work identifier, not independent chronology; a mula-versus-attha split is true by construction and is never cited as independent evidence.

pli-vism is shelved as mula by work role but counted as attha here by convention; it supplies 5 of the 25 attha defining-frame hits. Results are reported with and without it (attha defining-frame 20 of 25 without it); the direction is unchanged.

Magnitudes are per-character over the target subset, never raw rows. The attha and tika ratios rest on small denominators, so magnitude comparisons need bootstrap confidence intervals before they carry weight.
The qualitative first-appearance claim requires blind coding with Cohen kappa of at least 0.6 plus a 50-row application-sense audit; neither has been run, so that axis is open.
The Q9 diacritic zero-guard returned a statement-timeout error and did not complete; diacritic integrity is only indirectly verified and the guard must be re-run before reuse.
